excerpt: The First Intellect stands as the supreme emanation of divine grace, bearing witness to the Creator's unity.
source_location_hint: On the hierarchies of spiritual creation

excerpt: The Creator created the entirety of existence solely as an overflowing of grace, forgiveness, and reward.
source_location_hint: On the divine motivations for creation

excerpt: The essence of all worship is the affirmation of the Lord of all worlds, requiring the servant to know himself and his Creator.
source_location_hint: On the purpose of worship and creation

excerpt: The spiritual world was brought forth in a single, timeless instant from absolute non-existence into being, without any preexisting material or place.
source_location_hint: On the creation of the spiritual realm

excerpt: The spiritual world came into existence through the very emanation of the intellect; the Creator did not establish a separate domain but rather the spiritual world is constituted by the emergence of intellects.
source_location_hint: On the nature of the spiritual realm

excerpt: The Creator fashioned us free and autonomous, endowed with all perfections through the bestowal of knowledge, power, and life; we ourselves created bondage and dependence.
source_location_hint: On human nature and created freedom

excerpt: When the three letters are arranged in proper sequence, each positioned in its rightful place, they produce the meaningful utterance Lā ilāha illā Allāh, representing their actualized state.
source_location_hint: On the esoteric interpretation of the shahāda

excerpt: The three jewels were bestowed in a latent, potential state, and all beings were innocent yet capable of error in this condition.
source_location_hint: On the states of spiritual creation

excerpt: He was the whole vision of the whole, the whole hearing of the whole—the whole of sensation in its entirety.
source_location_hint: On the capacities of the spiritual intellects

excerpt: Although they possessed the power to consume refuse, their nature and reason revolted against it; the very perfection of their faculties precluded such an act.
source_location_hint: On the nature of infallibility and human choice

excerpt: The thing created by the Creator is greater than the thing created by the creature—secondary perfection acquired through tawhīd surpasses the primary perfection originally emanated.
source_location_hint: On the superiority of acquired excellence
